Implemented IdentifierNode refactoring from IdentifierResolutionPlan.md #857 (#861)

* Fix C++20 point-of-declaration: sizeof(x) in own initializer

Per [basic.scope.pdecl], a variable name is visible from the point
immediately after its declarator, before the initializer. This means
sizeof(x) in 'int x = sizeof(x)' is well-formed and evaluates to 4.

Root cause: three-part failure chain:
1. Parser pre-insertion missing: x was not in gSymbolTable when the
   initializer was parsed, so sizeof(x) was mis-parsed as sizeof of an
   unknown UserDefined type with size 0.
2. sizeof disambiguation: the UserDefined/size-0 check did not look up
   the symbol table to detect known variables.
3. Codegen pre-registration missing: even after fixing the parser to
   produce a from_expression sizeof node, the codegen's local symbol_table
   did not contain x when generateSizeofIr evaluated the initializer.

Changes:
- src/SymbolTable.h: add replace_variable() for updating a pre-inserted
  stub with its final node.
- src/Parser_Statements.cpp: in parse_variable_declaration(), pre-insert
  a stub VariableDeclarationNode (no init) for block-scope variables
  before parsing the initializer; replace with the real node after.
- src/Parser_Expr_PrimaryUnary.cpp: sizeof disambiguation now falls
  through to expression parsing when the identifier resolves to a
  VariableDeclarationNode in the symbol table.
- src/CodeGen_Stmt_Decl.cpp: register the variable in the codegen's
  local symbol_table before evaluating the initializer expression so
  that lookupDeclaration() succeeds during sizeof evaluation.
- tests/test_initializer_point_of_declaration_ret4.cpp: regression test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  (binding(), set_binding(), resolved_name(), set_resolved_name(), is_resolved())
- Add createBoundIdentifier(Token) inline helper to Parser.h that classifies
  Local/Global/Function/EnumConstant via ordinary symbol-table lookup; leaves

- Add get_scope_type_of_symbol() to SymbolTable.h for scope classification
- Update 16 identifier creation sites in Parser_Expr_PrimaryExpr.cpp to call
  createBoundIdentifier() instead of plain IdentifierNode(token)
- Fix typo: rename idenfifier_token -> identifier_token (308 occurrences)
- Add TDD overload-set tests (both pass with current compiler confirming

All 1327 tests pass.

* Identifier resolution Phase 1B: use binding() in codegen fast-paths

- generateIdentifierIr(): fast-path for Global binding avoids multi-table lookup
  cascade. Skips fast-path when using-declarations override name resolution
  (e.g., using ::globalValue inside a namespace) to ensure correctness.
- Assignment (=) handler: use binding() == Global to skip detectGlobalOrStaticVar;
  fall through to detectGlobalOrStaticVar for all other bindings (including Local
  which covers static locals with global storage).
- Compound-assignment (+=/-= etc.) handler: same binding-aware approach, plus
  direct global table lookup for type/size info.
- Inc/dec (++/--) handler: use binding() == Global fast-path, fall through
  to detectGlobalOrStaticVar for all other bindings.
- detectGlobalOrStaticVar() retained as fallback for Unresolved/Local bindings
  (handles StaticLocal and StaticMember which Phase 1A left as Unresolved/Local).

All 1327 tests pass.

* docs: add NON_STANDARD_BEHAVIOR.md cataloguing 33 C++20 deviations

Documents all known places where FlashCpp deviates from the ISO C++20
standard, grouped into 9 categories:
- Lookup/overload: ADL missing, free-function ops invisible, first-overload
  fallback, mangling wrong-type fallbacks
- Lambda: capture-all by odr-use, constexpr capture limits
- Templates: NTTP deduction, silent instantiation fallback
- Constexpr: constructor body ignored, consteval unenforced
- ABI: SysV struct passing, pointer_depth=0 in 7+ sites
- Exception handling: __cpp_exceptions absent, cross-refs EXCEPTION_HANDLING.md
- Preprocessor: _Pragma partial, __GNUC__+_MSC_VER both defined, macro lies
- Codegen: synthesised ops return false, new[] skips elements, InternalError

Cross-references existing docs for the 18 already-documented items.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Phase 2 — set CapturedByValue/CapturedByRef bindings at parse time

Add lambda_capture_stack_ to Parser to track explicit named captures
([x], [&x]) while parsing lambda bodies. createBoundIdentifier() now
upgrades Local bindings to CapturedByValue or CapturedByRef when the
identifier name appears in the current capture stack.

Changes:
- Parser.h: add lambda_capture_stack_ field; update createBoundIdentifier()
  to check capture stack at both DeclarationNode and VariableDeclarationNode
  Local-binding sites
- Parser_Expr_ControlFlowStmt.cpp: push/pop explicit capture map around
  parse_block() in parse_lambda_expression()
- CodeGen_Expr_Primitives.cpp: generateIdentifierIr() uses binding() for
  explicit captures; retains runtime capture_kinds fallback for [=]/[&]
  capture-all variables (which keep Local binding)
- CodeGen_Expr_Operators.cpp: captured-by-ref assignment checks binding()
  first, falls back to runtime capture_kinds map for capture-all captures
- tests/test_lambda_this_implicit_member_ret0.cpp: new TDD regression test
  for [this] capture with implicit member access (no this->)

All 1328 tests pass (1326 runtime, 26/26 _fail).

* feat: Phase 4A — basic namespace ADL per C++20 [basic.lookup.argdep]

- Add SymbolTable::lookup_adl() that collects candidates from
  associated namespaces (struct namespace + base class namespaces)
- Integrate ADL into the legacy unqualified call path in
  Parser_Expr_PrimaryExpr.cpp: after parsing args, try ADL when
  ordinary lookup only produced a fake forward declaration, then
  return early so the proper FunctionCallNode is not overwritten
- Also extend is_function_call condition to allow the proper
  overload-resolution path to fire when ordinary lookup finds nothing
- ADL is suppressed when ordinary lookup found a non-function decl
- Tests: test_adl_namespace_function_ret0.cpp (direct namespace ADL),

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Phase 4B - hidden friend ADL for inline friend functions

Inline friend function definitions inside a class/struct body are now
findable via ADL (and also via ordinary unqualified lookup - see
NON_STANDARD_BEHAVIOR.md) instead of being silently skipped.

Key changes:
- SymbolTable.h: add insert_into_namespace() to register a function
  directly into namespace_symbols_ for ADL to find it
- Parser.h: add is_free_function flag to DelayedFunctionBody and
  pending_hidden_friend_defs_ vector
- Parser_FunctionBodies.cpp: skip member_function_context_stack_
  push/pop when is_free_function == true (no 'this' for free friends)
- Parser_Decl_StructEnum.cpp: parse_friend_declaration() now
  (a) uses parse_parameter_list() instead of a raw paren-depth skip,
  (b) for non-operator, non-member inline friend bodies: creates a
      FunctionDeclarationNode, computes its mangled name, queues its
      body for delayed parsing, and registers it in namespace_symbols_
- Parser_Decl_TopLevel.cpp: consume pending_hidden_friend_defs_ in
  both the top-level struct handler and the namespace body loop so
  the IR converter generates code for the functions
- docs/NON_STANDARD_BEHAVIOR.md: update §1.1 to reflect ADL is now
  implemented; add §1.1a documenting the hidden-friend/ordinary-lookup
  non-standard widening

New test: tests/test_adl_hidden_friend_ret0.cpp
  namespace Lib { struct X { int val; friend int getVal(X x) { return x.val; } }; }
  int main() { Lib::X x{0}; return getVal(x); }
  ADL finds Lib::getVal through the argument type Lib::X.

* Preserve using-imported overload sets

Teach SymbolTable lookup to collect ALL candidates from all using-imported
scopes and merge them into an overload set, rather than returning the first
match.

Changes in SymbolTable.h:
- In lookup() and lookup_all(): check scope.symbols before
  using_declarations_handles, since using declarations are now fully
  materialized into the symbols map.
- Replace the single-overload materialization in add_using_declaration()
  with materialize_using_declaration_symbols() which merges into the
  existing overload set when both sides are pure function sets.
- Change lookup_all_using_directives_first() to merge overloads across all
  using-directive namespaces instead of returning the first match.
- Add helper functions: is_pure_function_set, contains_same_function_decl,
  append_unique_function_overloads, materialize_using_declaration_symbols.

Fixes:
- test_using_declaration_overload_set_ret0.cpp (was returning 1, now 0)
- test_using_directive_overload_merge_ret0.cpp (was returning 1, now 0)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

auto params are not in scope when the trailing return type is parsed, so

* Implement Phase 3 deferred items: dependent-base guard and post-substitution rebind

- Parser.h: add has_deferred_base_classes guard in bindNonStaticMemberFromContext
  lambda inside createBoundIdentifier(). When parsing a template body and the
  current struct has dependent (deferred) base classes, skip gLazyMemberResolver

  for eager binding; concrete-base names stay Unresolved for instantiation-time
  resolution.

- AstNodeTypes_DeclNodes.h: add const Token& identifier_token() const getter to
  IdentifierNode (parallel to QualifiedIdentifierNode which already had it).

- Parser_Templates_Substitution.cpp: inline post-substitution re-binding in
  substituteTemplateParameters(). After template-parameter matching, any
  Unresolved IdentifierNode is re-tried via createBoundIdentifier(). If the
  result is a non-member binding (Global/Local/Function/EnumConstant/StaticLocal)
  the updated node is returned, enabling fast-path codegen paths instead of the
  runtime Unresolved fallback.

- tests: add test_template_concrete_and_dependent_base_member_ret42 (guard smoke
  test) and test_template_rebind_late_global_ret0 (rebind smoke test).

  update Files Summary table; revise progress note to reflect 1359 tests passing.

* Fix Ubuntu clang build break and remove leftover parser debug output (#862)

* Fix struct pointer inc/dec for local pointer variables

Port of FlashCpp2 commit 57a8ddce. Local pointer variables (P* p)
were not handled in generateBuiltinIncDec: the pointer operand was
extracted as IrValue{} (empty) and the store-back was silently
skipped when operandIrOperands[2] is a TempVar.

Fixes:
- Use toIrValue() for ptr_operand to handle both StringHandle and TempVar
- Add storeBackUpdatedValue lambda that handles both destinations
- Add getOperandPointerDepth/populateIncDecTypedValueMetadata helpers
- Propagate pointer_depth in analyzeAddressExpression for locals
- Always do the postfix save regardless of operand kind
- Token propagation for better debug info

Test: test_struct_ptr_incdec_ret70 (prefix ++p, postfix p++ on struct ptr)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix integer divide/modulo when divisor is in RAX

Port of FlashCpp2 commit dbe67b66. When the divisor expression
evaluates into RAX (e.g. a function return value), the subsequent
mov RAX, <dividend> overwrites it before IDIV, producing wrong
results (effectively RAX/RAX = 1).

Add three helpers to IRConverter_Conv_Arithmetic.h:
- reserveDivisionFixedRegisters(): extracted flush+lock of RAX/RDX
- allocateDivisionScratchRegister(): finds a scratch reg from
  RCX/R8-R11 avoiding specified registers
- preserveDivisorAcrossRaxMove(): if divisor_reg==RAX, copies it
  to a scratch register before the dividend-to-RAX move

Apply to handleDivide, handleUnsignedDivide, handleModulo,
handleDivAssign (restructured to check FP type before setup),
and handleModAssign. Also add missing regAlloc.release(RDX)
in handleModAssign.

Test: test_divide_divisor_in_rax_ret5 (25 / get_divisor() = 5)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix float conversion XMM reuse under register pressure

Port of FlashCpp2 commit 423edce9. In handleFloatToFloat, source_xmm
and result_xmm are allocated sequentially. Under XMM register pressure
the allocator may return the same register for both, causing
regAlloc.release(source_xmm) to free the result register before it
is stored.

Guard the release: only release source_xmm when it differs from
result_xmm.

Test: test_float_to_double_xmm_pressure_ret120 exercises float-to-double
conversion with 15 live float values to maximise XMM pressure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: only set is_global_or_static when global symbol lookup actually succeeds

The `is_global_or_static = true` was placed outside the `if (sym.has_value())`
block in analyzeAddressExpression, causing it to be set even when the global
symbol table lookup returned nullopt. This resulted in returning
AddressComponents with final_type=Void and final_size_bits=0, which could
cause incorrect memory accesses downstream in ComputeAddress generation.

The old detectGlobalOrStaticVar only set this flag when the lookup succeeded
AND the symbol had valid type information. This restores that safety check.

* Use optional<TemplateDepthGuard> instead of ScopedState + manual ++ for template depth

Replace the mixed ScopedState/manual-increment pattern with the idiomatic
conditional TemplateDepthGuard used everywhere else. Fixes indentation and
improves consistency. No behavioral change.

* Revert simple assignment StaticMember change: isValid() assignment is fine here since gsi has no type/size fields

* Enforce C++20 [temp.res]/9 Phase 1 two-phase name lookup

Non-dependent names used inside a template body must be visible at
template definition time. Using a name declared after the template
definition is now rejected as ill-formed.

Implementation:
- Parser.h: Added phase1_cutoff_line_, phase1_cutoff_file_idx_,
  phase1_violation_token_ fields. createBoundIdentifier() now calls
  checkPhase1 for global-scope FunctionDeclarationNode,
  TemplateFunctionDeclarationNode, DeclarationNode, and
  VariableDeclarationNode.
- Parser_Templates_Inst_Deduction.cpp: reparse_template_function_body()
  sets the cutoff from the saved template body opening-brace token and
  throws CompileError after parse if a violation was detected.
- Parser_Templates_Substitution.cpp: Reverted to promoting
  Unresolved -> concrete (the reparse path handles the real check;
  throwing here caused false positives for 'this', args_0, etc.).
- Parser_Templates_Lazy.cpp: Re-throw CompileError before the two
  catch(std::exception&) blocks that were swallowing it.
- main.cpp: Catch CompileError in the parse phase and exit 1.

Tests:
- Added test_template_nondependent_not_visible_fail.cpp
- Added test_template_rebind_late_global_fail.cpp
- Removed test_template_rebind_late_global_ret0.cpp (was testing
  the old non-standard MSVC-like behaviour)

All 1361 tests pass; 28 _fail tests fail as expected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Port constexpr static member binding fixes from FlashCpp2

Honor parser-bound static member identifiers in constexpr evaluation
before falling back to unqualified lookup. Fixes four cases:
- Simple static constexpr member (Box::value vs global value)
- Qualified function targets in constexpr calls
- Static member objects (inner.value uses Outer::inner)
- Nested static member objects

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix createBoundIdentifier misclassifying enum-typed variables as EnumConstant

DeclarationNode with Type::Enum can be either an enumerator constant (e.g., Red)
or a variable/parameter of enum type (e.g., `Color c`). The old code classified
both as EnumConstant. Now we verify via EnumTypeInfo::findEnumerator that the
identifier is actually an enumerator value before setting EnumConstant binding.
Variables/parameters of enum type correctly fall through to Local/Global.

* Port identifier binding parity fixes

Add the missing constexpr identifier-binding regression tests and backport the parser, constexpr, template, and static-initializer fixes needed to make FlashCpp's identifier-binding suite pass after auditing newer FlashCpp2 work.

commit.message:
perf(db): add 14 missing PostgreSQL indexes

Tables impactées et requêtes couvertes :
- phase_outcomes(pattern_id) — AVG(duration_s) WHERE pattern_id=? à chaque phase (engine.py)
- phase_outcomes(created_at) — purge / fenêtres temporelles
- epic_runs(created_at DESC) — cockpit 24h/7d windows (multiple queries)
- epic_runs(session_id) — UPDATE WHERE session_id=? à chaque phase (engine.py)
- epic_runs(status, created_at DESC) — cockpit failure counts par fenêtre
- team_fitness_history(technology, phase_type) — boards WHERE technology=?
  (idx_tfh_key commence par agent_id → inutilisable pour filtre tech seul)
- team_fitness_history(snapshot_date DESC) — évolution temporelle
- team_okr(technology, kpi_name) — UPDATE KPI current par contexte
- team_okr(team_key) — ORDER BY team_key, kpi_name
- agent_assignments(project_id) — WHERE project_id=? (seul PK = agent_id)
- agent_scores(agent_id, epic_id) — composite selection.py WHERE agent_id+epic_id
- messages(from_agent, timestamp DESC) — cockpit activity feed filtrée
- llm_traces(session_id, created_at DESC) — trace lookup par session
- tool_calls(agent_id, timestamp DESC) — fenêtres d'activité agent
+ rl_experience(created_at DESC) + admin_audit_log(resource_type, resource_id)

Indexes ajoutés dans migrations.py (existing DBs) ET schema_pg.sql (fresh installs).
Tous idempotents (CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: self-improving skill for all agents + perplexity web search with MCP Playwright fixes (#7)

Adds the `self-improving` skill to all five OpenClaw agents and enables
Perplexity-based web search for research-capable agents. Fixes the
`perplexity-webui-search` skill which used non-standard Playwright MCP
APIs throughout.

## Self-improving skill — all agents

Added `self-improving` to `skills` in every `openclaw.json` (ceo, pm,
tech-lead, biz-research, cost-controller).

Each agent's `AGENTS.md` updated with:
- Tiered memory guidance (`memory/` for factual continuity,
`~/self-improving/` for execution quality)
- Weekly heartbeat check to promote corrections and keep `memory.md` ≤
100 lines

## Perplexity web search — research agents

Added `perplexity-webui-search` to ceo, tech-lead, and biz-research
(`openclaw.json` + `AGENTS.md`).

## Perplexity skill — MCP Playwright compliance fixes

The skill was written against a non-existent selector-based API. Fixed
to use the standard snapshot-first `ref` pattern:

| Before (non-standard) | After (MCP standard) |
|---|---|
| `browser_navigate({ url, waitUntil })` | `browser_navigate({ url })` |
| `browser_type({ selector, text })` | `browser_snapshot()` →
`browser_type({ element, ref, text })` |
| `browser_press({ selector, key })` | `browser_press_key({ key })` |
| `browser_evaluate({ script })` | `browser_evaluate({ function })` |
| `browser_get_text({ selector })` | `browser_evaluate({ function })` |
| `browser_choose_file({ selector, files })` | `browser_file_upload({
paths })` |

Added a **MCP Playwright Tool Reference** section explaining the
snapshot-first workflow with a concrete example showing how to read
`ref` values from the accessibility tree. Replaced all LaTeX formatting
with standard Markdown.

## Perplexity skill — direct Playwright MCP setup (v1.2.0)

Replaced the ClawHub super-skill dependency
(`Spiceman161/playwright-mcp` via `@lobehub/market-cli`) with the
official **`@playwright/mcp`** package used directly:

- `requires.skills` dependency removed; replaced with `requires.bins:
[npx]`
- Prerequisites updated to `npm install -g @playwright/mcp@latest` with
an MCP server config JSON snippet
- Troubleshooting updated to use `npx playwright install chromium`

commit.message:
Phase 6.2: Implement PM4 command buffer packets for AMD GPUs

Implement PM4 (Packet Manager version 4) packet encoding for AMD RDNA2/3 GPUs.
PM4 is AMD's command submission format, equivalent to Intel's 3DSTATE commands.

Implementation:
- Created render-sys/src/pm4.rs with comprehensive PM4 packet builder (587 LOC)
- PM4Builder for Type 3 packet encoding and accumulation
- Core packets: NOP, SET_CONTEXT_REG, SET_SH_REG, SET_UCONFIG_REG
- Draw commands: DRAW_INDEX_AUTO with primitive topology support
- Synchronization: EVENT_WRITE, SURFACE_SYNC, ACQUIRE_MEM, RELEASE_MEM
- Pipeline control: CLEAR_STATE, SET_BASE
- Coherency control flags for cache management (coher_cntl, gcr_cntl modules)
- PrimitiveType enum for topology (PointList, LineList, TriangleList, etc.)

Testing:
- 14 comprehensive PM4 packet tests (100% pass rate)
- Total: 215 Rust tests passing
- All Go tests passing (including race detector)
- Static linking verified

Compliance:
- Zero regressions in complexity/duplication metrics
- Follows project patterns (similar to Intel CommandBuilder)
- Reference: Mesa RADV, AMD register databases

Phase 6.2 complete. Ready for Phase 6.3 (AMD ISA Backend).

commit.message:
Add procedural Shakespeare play system with mood-driven music and visuals

- 200-line Shakespeare corpus tagged by emotion (10 categories) and tone (5 types)
- 6 three-act arc templates (Rivalry, Romance, Tragedy, Comedy, Dream, Trial)
- Procedural play generator: character tags + backdrop seed unique scripts
- Cue cards floating above cabinet with fade-in/hold/drift-out lifecycle
- Mood-driven pacing: conflict/madness fast, farewell/despair slow
- Color suffusion: speaking character's card tints, backdrop shifts
- Mood-shifted kalimba music: register, rate, volume vary by emotion
- Structural stings: ascending arpeggio (title), root+fifth (acts), descending (fin)
- Emotion glyphs: particles (hearts, curses, stars, dots) float from speakers
- Vinyl wind-down: puppets decelerate after Fin
- Shift+crank to rewind play and puppet movement
- Title/Fin cards with 6 randomized title formats
- 32 stage directions (8 opening + 24 mid-play) with no-repeat logic
- 2 new characters: Princess (royal/gentle), Hag (dark/wild)
- Updated CLAUDE.md with full architecture documentation
